note:
只要看一眼沃尔玛最便宜的笔记本电脑EVOO EV-C-116-5的规格，任何技术爱好者都会清楚地知道，这款设备并不会真正成为一款强大的设备。但这款小小的笔记本电脑只卖139美元，而且它的数据似乎与200-250美元的Chromebook不相上下。所以最近，我付了信用卡，订了一张。穿着紫色的衣服。
在我们亚洲人中，EVOO显然不会是Windows笔记本电脑的好选择。由于只有2G的RAM，EVOO无法在不消耗虚拟内存的情况下运行任何东西(将数据从RAM交换到磁盘，然后再交换回来)。除此之外，32 GB的固态硬盘根本不足以容纳Windows本身，更不用说任何应用程序了。当EVOO第一次尝试升级到Windows 10的新版本(例如，上个月刚刚发布的Windows 10 Build 2004)时，会因为空间不足而失败。
不过，这并不一定意味着笔记本电脑就完全没用了。也许可以用Linux重新加载它--甚至像Ubuntu这样的重量级发行版在RAM和存储上都比Windows容易得多。见鬼，甚至认为一台2G内存、32 GB固态硬盘的笔记本电脑不能很好地运行Windows10的假设也是不太正确的。只有一种方法可以找出答案。
note_en:
One glance at the specs on Walmart's cheapest laptop—the  EVOO EV-C-116-5—makes it clear to any technical enthusiast that the device is not exactly going to be a powerhouse. But the little laptop only costs $139, and its stats appear to stack up well with  $200-$250 Chromebooks. So recently, I ponied up my credit card and ordered one. In purple.
 Amongst us Arsians, it's obvious that the EVOO is not going to be a good choice for a Windows laptop. With only 2GiB of RAM, the EVOO won't be able to run anything without hammering virtual memory (swapping data from RAM to disk and back again). Beyond that, a 32GB SSD simply is not enough room for Windows itself, let alone any applications. The first time the EVOO tries to upgrade to a new build of Windows 10 (for example, Windows 10 build 2004, which just released last month), it will fail due to lack of space.
 That doesn't necessarily mean the laptop is entirely useless, though. Perhaps it could be reloaded with Linux—even "heavyweight" distributions like  Ubuntu are considerably easier on both RAM and storage than Windows is. Heck, maybe even the assumption that a 2GiB RAM, 32GB SSD laptop can't run Windows 10 well isn't quite right. There was only one way to find out.
title: 我们买了沃尔玛140美元的笔记本电脑，这样你就不必
title_en: We bought Walmart’s $140 laptop so you wouldn’t have to

note:
这本新版的“序的艺术”包含了许多重要的变化。每章末尾的大部分背景部分都进行了更新，以考虑到最近的重要研究成果，参考资料得到了极大的扩展，并增加了更多已成功用于本课程教学的高级练习。
第二部分，即PROLOG语言，为了与新的PROLOG标准兼容而进行了修改，关于程序开发的章节也有了很大的改变：定义的谓词被移到了更合适的章节，关于效率的章节被移到了相当广泛的关于删减和否定的章节，并且增加了一个关于逐步增强的新章节-一种构造由Leon Sterling开发的PROLOG程序的系统方法。除了第三部分“高级序言编程技术”中的一个章节之外，所有的章节都有了实质性的改变，并进行了一些重大的重新安排。关于解释器的新一章描述了一种用于专家系统的规则语言和解释器，它更好地说明了如何使用PROLOG来构造专家系统。关于程序转换的章节是全新的，关于逻辑语法的章节增加了识别简单语言的新材料，展示了语法如何应用于更多的计算机科学示例。
note_en:
This new edition of  The Art of Prolog contains a number of important changes. Most background sections at the end of each chapter have been updated to take account of important recent research results, the references have been greatly expanded, and more advanced exercises have been added which have been used successfully in teaching the course.
 Part II, The Prolog Language, has been modified to be compatible with the new Prolog standard, and the chapter on program development has been significantly altered: the predicates defined have been moved to more appropriate chapters, the section on efficiency has been moved to the considerably expanded chapter on cuts and negation, and a new section has been added on stepwise enhancement—a systematic way of constructing Prolog programs developed by Leon Sterling. All but one of the chapters in Part III, Advanced Prolog Programming Techniques, have been substantially changed, with some major rearrangements. A new chapter on interpreters describes a rule language and interpreter for expert systems, which better illustrates how Prolog should be used to construct expert systems. The chapter on program transformation is completely new and the chapter on logic grammars adds new material for recognizing simple languages, showing how grammars apply to more computer science examples.
title: “序曲的艺术”(1994)

note:
Basecamp首席执行官Jason Fry写了一封公开信，解释他对苹果App Store支付政策的真正问题是什么。他说，这不仅仅是钱的问题--尽管他承认这是“故事的一大部分”--而是“关于没有选择，以及苹果如何强行将自己插入公司和客户之间”。
这里有一些促使他写公开信的背景：Basecamp最近发布了一项名为“嘿”的多平台电子邮件服务，每年的费用至少为99美元。要使用它，你必须在网上支付订阅费。这家科技巨头对此并不满意，它拒绝了公司在嘿发布后不久试图推出的一个漏洞修复。苹果在驳回对其决定的上诉后发给Basecamp的一封信中表示，开发者必须提供应用内购买，作为客户解锁特性和功能的一种方式。
正如你可能知道的，苹果对付费应用和任何应用内购买收取30%的佣金。许多开发者对这个词不满意，这已经不是什么秘密了--由于Spotify和乐天旗下的电子阅读器应用Kobo的投诉，欧盟委员会甚至对App Store和Apple Pay展开了双重调查。Spotify首席执行官丹尼尔·埃克(Daniel Ek)很久以前就透露，该公司负担不起苹果的费用。在欧盟委员会宣布调查后，叮当的母公司Match Group和所有者堡垒之夜也批评了这家科技巨头收取部分收益。
苹果表示，如果修改嘿电子邮件应用程序，它只会推出Basecamp试图提交的错误修复。在接受TechCrunch采访时，这家科技巨头的高级副总裁菲尔·席勒(Phil Schiller)表示，公司没有计划改变政策来适应开发者。事实上，席勒说，嘿iOS应用程序被错误地批准了。他还表示，Basecamp本可以通过不同的方式，比如在应用程序和网络上收取不同的价格，在目前的规则下让嘿应用程序可以接受。弗里德在信中称这一声明为“反垄断金”，因为这大概意味着Basecamp应该向iOS用户收取更高的费用。
这位首席执行官还阐述了他所说的“没有选择”是什么意思。他说，如果嘿的客户通过App Store注册，Basecamp将不能再提供“退款、信用卡换卡、折扣、试用延期、困难例外、补偿、部分付款、非营利性折扣、教育折扣、停机时间抵免、免税等”。例如，它不能像它的项目管理软件客户那样延长试用或付款期限给那些需要它的人。或者为像急救人员这样的人提供免费版本。
此外，它将无法将支付信息从一个平台转移到另一个平台。如果客户突然不得不将平台从iOS切换到Android，那么他们最终可能会无法访问他们的电子邮件地址和收件箱中的所有东西。“这就是为什么我们有一个通用的、不特定于平台的集中计费系统，”他写道。弗里德在信的结尾这样写道：
“那么我们想要什么呢？我不是说IAP不应该存在，或者不应该是一种选择。对于一些企业来说，这可能是有意义的。如果苹果把你所有的客户都送给你，这可能是有意义的。正如国会议员西西林最近在一次采访中所说，30%的比率仍然是高速公路抢劫案，但对我们来说，根本问题是缺乏选择。
苹果，请给你的开发者一个选择！让我们通过我们自己的系统为我们的客户开具账单，这样我们就可以用我们自己的方式帮助他们延长、退款、折扣或任何其他方式。这是我们的事，不是你的事。菲尔·席勒建议我们应该提高iOS客户的价格，以弥补苹果增加的利润率，这是反垄断金。
note_en:
Basecamp CEO Jason Fried has  written an open letter explaining what his real problem is with Apple’s App Store payment policies. He said it’s not just about the money — though he admits that it’s “a large part of the story” — but “about the absence of choice and how Apple forcibly inserts themselves between [the] company and [its] customer.”
 Here’s a little background on what prompted his open letter: Basecamp recently released a multi-platform email service called “Hey,” which costs at least $99 a year. To be able to use it, you’ll have to pay for subscription on the web. That didn’t sit well with the tech giant, which rejected a bug fix the company tried to roll out shortly after Hey’s launch. In a letter Apple sent to Basecamp after rejecting an appeal to its decision, it said developers must offer in-app purchasing as a way for customers to unlock features and functionality.
  As you may know, Apple takes a 30 percent commission on paid apps and any in-app purchase. It’s no secret that a lot of developers aren’t happy with the term — the European Commission is even  opening dual investigations into the App Store and Apple Pay due to complaints filed by Spotify and, based on reports, Rakuten-owned e-reader app Kobo. Spotify chief Daniel Ek revealed long ago that the company can’t afford Apple’s fees. And after the EC announced its probe, Tinder’s parent company Match Group and  Fortnite owner Epic Games also  criticized the tech giant for collecting a portion of their earnings.
 Apple said it will only roll out the bug fix Basecamp tried to submit if it revises the Hey email app. In an  interview with   TechCrunch, the tech giant’s Senior Vice President Phil Schiller said the company has no plans to change its policies to accommodate the developer. In fact, Schiller said the Hey iOS app was approved in error. He also said that Basecamp could have made the Hey app acceptable under current rules through different ways, such as charging different prices in the app and on the web. Fried called that statement “antitrust gold” in his letter, since that presumably suggests that Basecamp should charge its iOS customers more.
 The CEO also expounded on what he meant by “absence of choice.” He said that if Hey’s customers sign up through the App Store, Basecamp will no longer be able to offer “refunds, credit card changes, discounts, trial extensions, hardship exceptions, comps, partial payments, non-profit discounts, educational discounts, downtime credits, tax exceptions, etc.” It can’t extend trials or payment terms for those who need it like it does for its project management software customers, for instance. Or offer free versions to people like first responders.
 Further, it won’t be able to transfer payment information from one platform to another. If a customer suddenly has to switch platforms from iOS to Android, then they may end up losing access to their email address and everything in their inbox. “This is why we have a universal, non-platform-specific centralized billing system,” he wrote. Fried ended his letter with this:
 “So what do we want? I’m not saying IAP shouldn’t exist, or shouldn’t be an option. For some businesses, it might make sense. If Apple is sending you all your customers, it probably does make sense. The 30% rate is still highway robbery, as Congressman Cicilline recently said in an interview, but the fundamental problem for us is the lack of choice.
  Apple, please just give your developers the choice! Let us bill our own customers through our own systems, so we can help them with extensions, refunds, discounts, or whatever else our own way. It’s our business, not your business. And Phil Schiller’s suggestion that we should raise prices on iOS customers to make up for Apple’s added margin is antitrust gold.”
title: Basecamp首席执行官表示，苹果应用商店的问题关乎“没有选择”
title_en: Basecamp CEO says Apple App Store issue is about 'absence of choice'

note:
一家太空初创公司周四宣布，研究人员、坐在扶手椅上的宇航员，甚至是寻求非凡婚礼体验的新娘和新郎，都将能够在10万英尺高空的气球升空加压舱内庆祝、收集数据或简单地欣赏风景，舱内配有酒吧和洗手间。
“海王星”号宇宙飞船由一家名为“太空透视”的公司运营，位于美国宇航局肯尼迪航天中心的租赁设施中，每次将搭载8名乘客，飞行时间为6个小时。客舱由一个巨大的充氢气球抬起，将以每小时12英里的平稳速度攀升至约30英里高的高度。随后将缓慢降落到大西洋，在那里将有一艘打捞船待命，以确保船舱和船员的安全。
搭载科研有效载荷的试飞预计将于2021年开始。预计在未来三年半左右的时间里，第一批搭载乘客的航班将进行试飞，在此之前将进行试点试飞。
虽然该公司最初将在佛罗里达航天港运营，但该系统可能会从世界各地的多个地点发射，夏威夷和阿拉斯加近期可能会发射该系统。
载人航班的票价尚未确定，但该公司官员周四表示，初始成本可能在每位乘客12.5万美元左右。这大约是太空游客乘坐火箭动力太空飞机(如维珍银河正在开发的飞机，设计高度超过50英里)进行亚轨道飞行所需支付的一半左右。
在这个高度下，太空船“海王星”将飞行得很好，乘客不会经历失重，但它们仍将位于地球大气层的99%以上，几乎是超音速协和式飞机曾经飞行高度的两倍。与只在轨道顶端停留几分钟的较短的亚轨道火箭飞行不同，海王星的乘客将在高峰高度享受两个小时的时间，通过大的环绕式窗户欣赏风景。
我们在设计中所做的最令人惊叹的事情之一就是能够举办各种活动，比如婚礼、公司活动等。空间透视创始人兼联合首席执行官泰伯·麦卡勒姆(Taber MacCallum)说，我迫不及待地想看到精神领袖与政治领袖一起飞行。
我想我们会看到各种各样的航班。科学飞行将会非常有趣，将旅游和科学结合在一起。想象力无处不在。我们对所有类型的伟大想法都产生了浓厚的兴趣。
太空透视公司已经与美国宇航局签署了一项太空法案协议，该公司已经安排租赁3英里长的跑道上的一个设施，这条跑道曾经供返航的航天飞机使用。海王星的飞行将由美国联邦航空局商业航天办公室管理。
佛罗里达太空公司总裁兼首席执行官弗兰克·迪贝洛在一份声明中表示，太空视角将为开普敦带来全新的能力，这将增强我们在佛罗里达州提供的与太空相关的研究和旅游服务。它在佛罗里达的存在不仅创造了就业和供应链机会，也为民用宇航员提供了从太空边缘体验地球的机会，这一特权以前只有少数人享有。
麦卡勒姆和联席首席执行官简·波因特(Jane Poynter)表示，广泛的市场研究显示，广大用户对此类航班的兴趣尚未开发。
波因特在一次电话会议上说，当我们把所有想要带到太空边缘的人带到太空边缘时，我们希望他们能够真正体验宇航员们谈论的东西，在太空中看到地球(并且)舒适、温和和平易近人地做这件事。
一次将有八个人，太空舱里有一名机组人员。当然，你也可以与你在当地的朋友联系。我们还会有一些非常棒的通讯系统，这样我们就可以在那里举办各种现场活动了。整个太空舱设计得非常灵活，可以让各种东西在太空环境中上下活动。
至于婚礼，她说海王星将提供有史以来最好的结婚地点。
note_en:
Researchers, armchair astronauts and even brides and grooms looking for an out-of-this-world wedding experience will be able to celebrate, collect data or simply enjoy the view from an altitude of 100,000 feet in a balloon-borne pressurized cabin, complete with a bar and a restroom, a  space startup announced Thursday.
  "Spaceship Neptune," operated by a company called Space Perspective from leased facilities at NASA's Kennedy Space Center, will carry eight passengers at a time on six-hour flights. The passenger cabin, lifted by a huge hydrogen-filled balloon, will climb at a sedate 12 mph to an altitude of about 30 miles high. That will be followed by a slow descent to splashdown in the Atlantic Ocean where a recovery ship will be standing by to secure the cabin and crew.
 Test flights carrying scientific research payloads are expected to begin in 2021. The first flights carrying passengers are expected within the next three-and-a-half years or so, with piloted test flights before that.
  While the company initially will operate out of the Florida spaceport, the system could be launched from multiple sites around the world, with Hawaii and Alaska near-term possibilities.
  Ticket prices for crewed flights have not yet been set, but company officials said Thursday the initial cost will probably be in the neighborhood of $125,000 per passenger. That's about half what space tourists can expect to pay for sub-orbital flights aboard   rocket-powered spaceplanes like those being   developed by Virgin Galactic, which are designed to reach altitudes of more than 50 miles.
 Spaceship Neptune will fly well under that altitude and passengers will not experience weightlessness, but they will still be above 99% of Earth's atmosphere, nearly twice as high as the supersonic Concorde once flew. And unlike shorter sub-orbital rocket flights that only spend a few minutes at the top of their trajectory, Neptune passengers will enjoy two hours at peak altitude, taking in the view through large, wrap-around windows.
 "One of the amazing things about the design we'e been able to work up is the ability to have events, things like weddings, corporate events. I can't wait to see spiritual leaders flying with political leaders," said Space Perspective founder and co-CEO Taber MacCallum.
  "I think we're going to see a wide variety of flights. Science flights are going to be really interesting, mixing tourism and science. The imagination runs wild. We're getting lots of interest in all types of great ideas."
 Space Perspective has signed a Space Act Agreement with NASA and the company has arranged to lease a facility at the 3-mile-long runway once used by returning space shuttles. Neptune flights will be regulated by the FAA's Office of Commercial Spaceflight.
  "Space Perspective is bringing a fundamentally new capability to the Cape, which will enhance the offering we have in Florida for space-related research and tourism," Space Florida President and CEO Frank DiBello said in a statement. "Its presence here in Florida creates not just job and supply chain opportunities, but opportunities for civilian astronauts to experience this planet Earth from the edge of space, a privilege previously available to only a few."
 MacCallum and co-CEO Jane Poynter said extensive market research showed untapped interest in such flights across a broad spectrum of users.
  "When we take all the people that we want to take to the edge of space, we want them to really be able to experience what astronauts talk about, seeing the Earth in space (and) doing it comfortably, gently and accessibly," Poynter said during a teleconference.
 "There'll be eight people at a time, with a crew member in the capsule. And of course, you'll be able to connect with your friends on the ground. And we'll have some really great communication systems so that we can have all kinds of live events up there as well. The whole capsule has been designed to be really flexible to allow for all kinds of things to go on and up in the space environment."
 As for weddings, she said Neptune will provide "the best place to get married, ever."
title: 太空初创公司承诺30英里高的气球将乘坐到“太空边缘”
title_en: Space Startup Promises 30-Mile-High Balloon Rides to the 'Edge of Space'

note:
我刚看完国际象棋24.com的马格努斯·卡尔森(Magnus Carlsen)旗下的一场快速在线国际象棋锦标赛，什么时候今天(一天后？)。我看到另一个锦标赛已经开始了。马格努斯自己也在比赛，还有其他世界级的球员。注意到马格努斯都是这些锦标赛的头号吸引力，他拥有这些锦标赛的股权，尽管是和其他投资者一起。
因此，我一直试图在我的脑海中为国际象棋服务的生产建模。
我首先要指出的是，观众比一周前的比赛更关心现场直播的新鲜比赛。当然，许多体育运动都是在同样的基础上进行的。
第二点是大多数棋手的时间机会成本相对较低，罗格夫和卡斯帕罗夫除外，再加上一些棋手可以为了盈利而代打扑克(而且可能已经退出了象棋)。事实上，他们在业余时间做的事情就是…。下棋！他们经常在一起下棋，而且经常在网上下棋。所以，如果你提出给他们一些钱，因为他们做了基本相同的事情，他们就会签约。特别是在大流行期间，他们中的许多人都被困在相对严格的隔离之下。
国际象棋选手一年中可以下很多天，也许不是每天，但你真的可以下很多棋，而不会撕裂你的肩袖。
那么，均衡似乎是国际象棋锦标赛的供应要高得多，特别是因为在线游戏消除了一些以前的进入障碍，比如需要一个场地和一些有形的基础设施。
你甚至可能最终得到一种马尔萨斯均衡，在这种均衡中，供应不断扩大，以满足相当低的边际成本。
但这是一种“超级巨星”式的竞争，因此回报将流向稀缺因素。稀缺因素是卡尔森本人，他比其他任何球员都更受关注。正如前面提到的那样，他是这家企业的股东，作为一名球员，他一直在赢得#1奖金。随着时间的推移，你可能会预计其他一些球员的回报-可能在前十名中，但不是那么有名或不那么有魅力-会接近马尔萨斯的水平。也许很多公众并不关心马格森是否会。在任何情况下，他们之间只有几个评级点相距不远。
注意到马格努斯·卡尔森(Magnus Carlsen)对声誉和互联网生产有多么了解。他一直在YouTube上发布“戏谑闪电战”的视频，视频中他在网上下速度棋，并在游戏进行过程中发表评论。他戏剧性地增加了国际象棋锦标赛的供应，他从中赚取收入。他已经是“稀缺因素”，而且他大幅扩大了针对他的关注供应。他明白，成功的互联网生产是频繁的生产。
在线观看国际象棋的人数(纽约时报)随着这种流行病的流行而上升，这也是因为这些努力。
别小看马格努斯·卡尔森，他在古典象棋、快棋和闪电战中都是第一名，他是YouTube国际象棋的巨星，他赢得了一场关于国际象棋琐事的锦标赛，他一直是全世界梦幻足球的第一名(这不是一件容易的壮举)。
现在，他正在为国际象棋带来一场经济革命，同时他自己也是头号劳动力和股权赢家。
他们只需要一个系统来确保没有作弊，超越了荣誉准则和今天使用的任何概率标准。
他们做得很好：任何有真金白银的锦标赛都会在任何时候对每个球员都有多个摄像头。最重要的是，顶级锦标赛只有受邀方可参加，而且不会吸引世界前50名选手以外的任何人：真的，即使是前20名，顶多也是如此。与赢得奖金相比，前20名选手因被抓而终身名誉损失的损失要大得多。
在快速和闪电战中作弊尤其困难，因为完美的电脑游戏与一个超级总经理在这种时间压力下能做的事情之间的差异是如此之大：每个人都会有重大的不准确，或者彻头彻尾的错误。最好的计算机动作需要技术计算，人类可能需要10分钟的思考，所以祝你好运，因为他们在15秒内就看到了。这不是一个概率标准的问题：即使是一个有引擎的俱乐部级别的球员也能判断出一步棋是非常好的还是不人道的，而今天的计算机在给出人类水平的建议方面并不是那么好。莉拉看起来可能比斯托克菲什更有人情味，但任何有一定技能的人都能判断出，对于没有引擎的人来说，什么时候行动太过鲁莽，就会告诉他们，整棵树的战术复杂性总是会出现在正确的位置。
这并不意味着没有作弊行为：在线国际象棋中有很多作弊行为。但在那些顶级赛事中这样做是愚蠢的。
南亚人想出了一种吸引人的方式来加快板球比赛的速度，并使板球在本世纪成为印度一项巨大的观众运动，因此想必存在一个下棋的最佳速度，以最大限度地提高观众的兴趣。动作间隔30分钟可能不是这样的。
第一届T20锦标赛大约在2003年在英国举行，目标是每场比赛不超过一场足球比赛。印度的IPL(板球在那里已经非常受欢迎)向来自世界各地的球员扔钱，并中了大奖。
马格努斯总是让我惊讶，不仅是他非凡的象棋才华，还有他的个性。作为这项运动的大使，我想说他是有史以来最好的世界冠军。他既超凡脱俗，又平易近人。有这么多顶级的在线巡回赛，而且大多数都是快速格式的，这真是太好了。这对网上来说是完美的，因为闪电战对评论员来说有点困难，他们不总是跟得上。对于观众来说，闪电战在他以戏谑的形式玩耍并进行自己的评论时表现得很好。这很奇怪，但我甚至不喜欢用真正的棋盘玩真正的游戏。网上购物要方便得多。我喜欢平板屏幕和鼠标，大部分时间是2分钟的游戏。有时比赛结束后，我会把我的位置贴到另一个有引擎的窗口里，看看我哪里出了问题。没有比现在更适合当棋手的时候了。
马格努斯所做的事情令人惊叹，但电子竞技才是行动、金钱和关注的地方。
很明显，国际象棋选手受到了两周棋手赚取的巨额奖金的影响。
世界排名前20的Nakamura目前正在发布大量的流媒体内容。你也注意到了卡尔森的调侃闪电战。
我认为均衡是那些拥有最大魅力和天赋的玩家最终会播放很多闪电战和子弹游戏，就像你说的那样，卡尔森目前是这里的第一名。如果他每周播放几次Twitch，我会很高兴地订阅他的视频，有数十万人观看他的戏谑闪电战和其他YouTube帖子，所以我认为他可以通过Twitch Grind在这里释放出可观的收入。
当然，中村比古典音乐更擅长闪电战，所以这些变化对他有利。
除了马格努斯·卡尔森是马格努斯·卡尔森的垄断供应商，因此有动机将马格努斯·卡尔森的供应限制在垄断水平。(特别是因为如果你有太多这样的锦标赛，它们就会失去魅力。客户愿意按单位付款不是固定的，而是在下降。)。
泰勒，我不明白你怎么写了这篇帖子而没有提到中村！从我的角度来看，这在经济上更吸引人。Naka在古典国际象棋方面在世界上排名第20，但在闪电战/子弹方面排名前2。他似乎已经决定，流媒体直播国际象棋娱乐对他来说比训练成为一名顶级古典棋手更好。你可以相当容易地估计他的Twitch收入。他有一个与众不同的产品，比大多数棋手(马格纳斯除外)具有更友好的互联网个性。这是一个更加经济有趣的故事，因为尽管法师很伟大，但他在国际象棋的所有方面都占据主导地位。他可以同时成为最好的在线棋手、最好的古典棋手、最好的象棋投资人。但是Naka&#39；的故事涉及稀缺性，在超级精英国际象棋技能和纯娱乐之间的有效边界上有一个独特的位置。
Hikaru也谈了很多关于投资的事情，显然开始有点行动了。不知道这能说明他的比较优势是什么。
也有很多非顶尖棋手似乎善于利用他们在网络上的存在--特别是那些魅力十足或口齿伶俐的人，或者两者兼而有之。
这里的市场有多大？有多少棋手能把这当做他们的日常工作呢？
是的，我来这里也是想对中卡提出类似的观点，但是你在这件事上做得很好。他的所作所为以及人们的一些反应都非常有趣(参见本·菲尼戈德几天前发表的非常犀利的评论)。
别忘了他来自Lichess.com在线闪电战锦标赛的流行视频！(他以Drunkenstein博士等化名参加比赛)
note_en:
I just finished watching  one of Chess24.com’s Magnus Carlsen-affiliated rapid on-line chess tournaments, when today (a day later?) I see that  another tournament has started.  And with Magnus himself playing, as well as other world-class players.  Note that Magnus both plays in these tournaments as the #1 attraction, and he owns an equity share in them, albeit with other investors.
 So I’ve been trying to model the production of chess services in my mind.
 I start with the point that viewers care much more about live, fresh games than games from a week ago.  Many sports of course operate on this same basis.
 The second point is that most chess players have a relatively low opportunity cost of time, Rogoff and Kasparov excepted, plus some chess players can substitute into poker for profit (and may have quit chess already).  In fact what they do in their spare time is to…play chess!  Often with each other, and often on-line.  So if you offer to pay them some amount for doing basically the same, they will sign up.  Especially during a pandemic when many of them are trapped under relatively severe quarantines.
 It is also the case that a chess player can play many days in the year, perhaps not every day, but you really can play a lot without tearing your rotator cuff.
 It then seems the equilibrium is a  much higher supply of chess tournaments, especially since on-line play removes some of the previous barriers to entry, such as needing a venue and some physical infrastructure.
 You might even end up with a kind of Malthusian equilibrium, where the supply keeps on expanding to meet a fairly low marginal cost.
 But this is a “superstars” kind of competition, and so the returns will go to the scarce factor.  That scarce factor is Carlsen himself, who garners far more attention than any other player.  And as noted he is an equity holder in this venture and as a player he has been winning the #1 prize money.  Over time, you might expect the returns of some of the other players — maybe in the top ten but not so famous or glamorous — to approach the Malthusian level.  Perhaps much of the public doesn’t care if Magnus plays #9 or #16, who in any case are only a small number of rating points apart.
 Notice how well Magnus Carlsen understands reputation and internet production.  He keeps on posting “ Banter Blitz” videos on YouTube, which show him playing speed chess on-line and commenting on the games as they proceed.  He dramatically expanded the supply of chess tournaments, which he earns income from.  He already was “the scarce factor,” and he has dramatically expanded the supply of attention aimed his way.  He understands that successful internet production is  frequent production.
 On-line chess viewing  is way up (NYT) with the pandemic, and also because of these efforts.
 Do not underestimate Magnus Carlsen.  He has been #1 in classical chess, rapid, and blitz, all at the same time.  He is a huge YouTube star in chess.  He has  won a tournament about chess trivia, and he has been  #1 in fantasy football  for the whole world (not an easy feat).
 And now he is bringing an economic revolution to chess, with himself as the #1 labor and equity earner at the same time.
  They just need a system to ensure no-cheating beyond the honour code and whatever probabilistic criteria used today.
  They do pretty well: Any tournament with real money has multiple cameras on every player at all times. On top of that, top tournaments are invitation only, and aren't going to bring in anyone outside of the top 50 players in the world: Really, even top 20, tops. A top 20 player has a lot more to lose in lifetime reputation loss by getting caught than they have to win in prize money.
 It's especially tough to cheat in rapid and blitz, as the difference between perfect computer play and what a super GM can do in such time pressures is so high: Everyone has major inaccuracies, or downright blunders. The best computer moves require technical calculation might take a human a 10 minute think, so good luck selling the fact that they saw it in 15 seconds. It's not a matter of probabilistic criteria: Even a club level player with an engine can tell when a move is very good or inhuman, and computers today aren't all that great at giving human level advice. Leela might look more human than Stockfish, but anyone with some skill can tell when a move is too rash for anyone that doesn't have an engine behind them telling them that entire trees of tactical complications are always going to come up right.
 That doesn't mean there's no cheating: There's a lot of cheating in online chess. But doing that in those premier tournaments would be silly.
  The South Asians figured out an appealing way to speed up cricket matches and made cricket a huge spectator sport in India in this century, so presumably there is an optimal speed at which chess should be played to maximize spectator interest. Thirty minutes between moves is probably not it.
  the first T20 tournament was in England in about 2003, the goal was for each match to be no longer than a football game. The IPL in India (where cricket was already insanely popular) threw money at players from all around the world and hit the jackpot.
  Magnus always amazes me, not just his extraordinary chess brilliance but his personality. As an ambassador for the sport , I’d say he is the best world champion that ever was. He is both otherworldly and approachable. It’s great that there are so many top online tourneys and most of them are in the rapid format. It’s perfect for online because blitz is a little tough for the commentators, they don’t always keep up well. For spectators, Blitz works well when he is playing in the banter format and doing his own commenting. It’s weird but I don’t even like playing a real game with a real board. Online is a lot more convenient. I like a flat screen and mouse and mostly 2 mins games. Sometimes I paste my position into another window with an engine right after the game to see where I went wrong. There has never been a better time to be a chess player.
  What Magnus is doing is amazing but e-sports is where the action, the money, and attention is.
  It's clear that chess players have been influenced by the megabucks being earned by Fortnight players.
 World top 20, Nakamura, is posting a huge amount of streaming content at the moment. And you've noted Carlsen's 'Banter Blitz'.
  I think the equilibrium is that the players that have the largest intersection of charisma and talent end up streaming a lot of blitz and bullet games, as you say Carlsen is the number 1 here by quite some way at the moment. I'd happily subscribe on Twitch if he streamed a few times a week, and a couple of hundred thousand people watch his Banter Blitz and other youtube posts so I think there is substantial revenue he can unlock here with the Twitch grind.
  Of course Nakamura is much better at blitz than classical so these changes favor him.
  Except that Magnus Carlsen is the monopoly supplier of Magnus Carlsen, and therefore has an incentive to restrict the supply of Magnus Carlsen to the monopoly level. (Particularly since these kinds of tournaments lose their glamour if you have too many of them. Customer willingness to pay is not fixed per unit, but declining.)
  Tyler, I do not understand how you wrote this post without mentioning Nakamura! Far more fascinating economically from my perspective. Naka is ~20th in the world in classical chess, but top 2 in blitz/bullet. He seems to have decided that streaming live chess entertainment is better for him than training to remain a top classical chess player. You can estimate his Twitch income fairly easily. He has a differentiated product, having a more internet-friendly personality than most chess players (Magnus excepted). It is a far more economically interesting story because Magus, great as he is, just dominates at everything in chess. He can be the best online player, the best classical player, the best chess investor, all at the same time. But Naka's story involves scarcity and a unique spot on the efficient frontier between super-elite chess skill and pure entertainment.
  Hikaru also talks a lot about investing, and apparently is starting to act a bit. Not sure what that says about his comparative advantage.
  There are also a lot of non-top players who seem good at leveraging their web presence in chess -- particularly those who are charming or articulate or both.
  How big is the market here? How many chess players can actually make this their day job?
  Yeah I came here to make a similar point about Naka but you've done a great job with this one. So interesting what he's doing, as well as some of the reactions (see Ben Finegold's really butthurt comments a couple days ago).
  Don't forget his popular videos from Lichess.com's online blitz tournaments! (He competed under the alias DrDrunkenstein among others)
title: 给国际象棋带来一场经济革命
title_en: Bringing an Economic Revolution to Chess

note:
如果不是冠状病毒，你会认为你当地的牙医诊所会做得很好。
与频繁开张和关门的餐厅不同，牙医诊所往往是稳定的业务，可以持续数十年。牙医收入可观-中位数为15.9万美元-而且提供的服务没有明确的替代品。如果你需要清洁牙齿或补牙，牙医是唯一的选择。
在一些经济学家看来，这使得它们成为衡量国家从流感大流行的冲击中复苏的完美晴雨表。
密歇根大学(University Of Michigan)经济学教授贝齐·史蒂文森(Betsey Stevenson)说：“如果你看看典型的牙医诊所，你会发现他们的商业模式没有任何问题。”“发生的只是冠状病毒。”
牙科行业经受住了大流行经济影响的夸大版本，与其他行业相比，它们经历了更陡峭的下滑和更快的复苏。由于各州关闭企业以减缓病毒的传播，一半的牙科工人在3月和4月失去了工作。根据非营利组织阿尔塔姆研究所(Altarum Institute)对联邦数据的分析，尽管该行业的工人仅占该行业的6%，但该行业在这几个月失去的所有医疗保健工作岗位中占了惊人的35%。
这些就业岗位需要多长时间才能完全恢复，这将是美国人是否感到安全、是否有经济手段恢复正常活动的关键指标。
史蒂文森说：“我痴迷于牙医，因为如果我们唯一要做的就是让经济暂停，然后恢复正常，那么他们都应该回来。”“在所有牙医都回去工作之前，我们并没有真正康复。”
3月16日，当疾病控制和预防中心(Centers For Disease Control And Prevention)和美国牙科协会(American Dental Association)发布反对选择性护理的联合指导意见时，牙科行业暂停了大部分工作。一些牙医说，由于防护设备供不应求，他们甚至很早就关门了。
根据牙科协会收集的数据，截至4月中旬，45%的牙医已经裁员。只有13%的办公室仍然完全开放，其余的办公室保持骨干员工。患者就诊次数降至正常比率的7%。
牙科协会的首席经济学家马尔科·武吉契奇(Marko Vujicic)预计，工人们回到牙医办公室的速度会很慢。但每两周向12000家牙科诊所发出的定期调查显示，恢复相对较快。
他说：“我最初的预测是，我们会乘电梯下来，再乘自动扶梯上去。”“但我们实际上看到就业岗位回升的速度相当快。”
到5月初，33%的牙科诊所已经重新聘用了全员。到5月中旬，这一数字上升到58%，最近在6月的第一周达到77%。
上周公布的新联邦数据也讲述了类似的故事。5月份，牙科行业增加了25万个就业岗位，占美国经济净新增就业岗位的整整10%。
联邦刺激计划可能在让牙医重返工作岗位方面发挥了关键作用。据估计，37%的牙科诊所通过Paycheck Protection Program获得资金，该计划旨在帮助小企业留住员工。参加该计划的牙医诊所比没有参加该计划的牙医诊所更有可能保持开放。
随着牙医返回工作岗位，目前还不清楚患者是否会跟随。虽然大多数州已经批准牙医诊所重新开业，但患者数量仍然是大流行前的一半。
这表明，导致患者取消预约的不仅仅是呆在家里的订单。一些人可能已经失去了他们过去在工作中获得的牙科保险。其他人可能害怕感染病毒；他们可能会觉得推迟已经等待了几个月的预防性护理更安全。或者他们可能会完全质疑定期清洁的价值。
牙医明白为什么走进他们的办公室--即使他们已经投资了额外的防护设备--可能也不是一个有吸引力的提议。
“你必须有人当着你的面，”杰森·巴斯蒂达(Jason Bastida)说，他主要在皇后区遭受冠状病毒重创的埃尔姆赫斯特(Elmhurst)社区执业。“我可以戴N95口罩，但你必须摘下口罩，让自己变得脆弱。”
根据英国科学家的说法，类固醇地塞米松是第一种被证明可以降低重病患者死亡率的治疗方法。这种药物似乎可以减少免疫系统引起的炎症，保护组织。在这项研究中，地塞米松使使用呼吸机的患者的死亡人数减少了三分之一，使用氧气的患者的死亡人数减少了五分之一。
冠状病毒紧急救援方案给了许多美国工人带薪假期，如果他们因为病毒需要休假的话。它给合格的工人两周的带薪病假，如果他们生病，被隔离，或者寻求冠状病毒的诊断或预防性护理，或者如果他们正在照顾生病的家庭成员。它给那些照顾孩子的人12周的带薪假期，这些孩子的学校关闭了，或者因为冠状病毒而无法提供托儿服务。这是美国第一次有广泛的联邦授权带薪休假，包括通常不会获得这种福利的人，比如兼职和零工经济工作者。但这项措施至少排除了一半的私营部门员工，包括美国最大雇主的员工，并给予小雇主拒绝休假的巨大回旋余地。
到目前为止，证据似乎表明确实如此。4月份发表的一篇被广泛引用的论文表明，人们在冠状病毒症状出现前两天左右传染性最强，并估计44%的新感染是由于尚未显示出症状的人传播的。最近，世界卫生组织的一位顶级专家表示，没有症状的人传播冠状病毒的情况“非常罕见”，但她后来收回了这一说法。
接触受污染的物体，然后将细菌感染我们自己，这并不是典型的病毒传播方式。但这是有可能发生的。对流感、鼻病毒、冠状病毒和其他微生物的多项研究表明，包括新型冠状病毒在内的呼吸道疾病可以通过接触受污染的表面传播，特别是在日托中心、办公室和医院等地方。但是，要让这种疾病以这种方式传播，必须发生一系列漫长的事件。保护自己免受冠状病毒感染的最好方法--无论是表面传播还是密切的人类接触--仍然是社交距离、洗手、不碰脸和戴口罩。
欧洲科学家的一项研究首次证明了基因变异和新冠肺炎(冠状病毒引起的疾病)之间存在强烈的统计联系。根据这项新的研究，拥有A型血的患者需要氧气或使用呼吸机的可能性增加了50%。
美国劳工部6月5日表示，5月失业率降至13.3%，这是美国就业市场的意外改善，因为就业反弹的速度快于经济学家的预期。4月份失业率达到14.7%，是二战后政府开始记录官方统计数据以来的最高水平，经济学家此前预测失业率将增加到20%。但失业率反而下降，雇主在4月份失去了2000多万个工作岗位后，又增加了250万个工作岗位。
在美国各地的城市，针对警察暴行的大规模抗议活动已导致数千人走上街头，这引发了人们对新一轮冠状病毒爆发的担忧，促使政治领导人、医生和公共卫生专家警告称，人群可能会导致病例激增。虽然许多政治领导人肯定了抗议者表达自己的权利，但他们敦促示威者戴上口罩，保持社会距离，这既是为了保护自己，也是为了防止病毒在社区进一步传播。一些传染病专家对抗议活动在户外举行这一事实感到放心，他们说，露天环境可以降低传播风险。
各州正在一点一滴地重新开放。这意味着有更多的公共空间可供使用，越来越多的企业被允许重新开业。联邦政府在很大程度上把决定权留给了各州，一些州的领导人把决定权留给了地方当局。即使你没有被告知呆在家里，限制外出旅行和你与其他人的互动仍然是一个好主意。
常见症状包括发烧、干咳、疲劳、呼吸困难或呼吸急促。这些症状中的一些与流感的症状重叠，使检测变得困难，但流鼻涕和鼻窦堵塞不太常见。疾控中心还增加了寒战、肌肉疼痛、喉咙痛、头痛和新的味觉或嗅觉丧失作为要注意的症状。大多数人在接触后五到七天就会生病，但症状可能会在短短两天或长达14天内出现。
如果航空旅行是不可避免的，你可以采取一些措施来保护自己。最重要的是：勤洗手，不要再摸你的脸。如果可能的话，选择靠窗的座位。埃默里大学的一项研究发现，在流感季节，坐在飞机上最安全的地方是靠窗的，因为坐在靠窗座位的人与可能生病的人接触较少。对坚硬的表面进行消毒。当你坐到座位上，双手干净后，用消毒湿巾清洁座椅的硬表面，如头枕和扶手、安全带扣、遥控器、屏幕、座椅靠背口袋和托盘桌子。如果座椅很硬，不透气，或者是皮革或人造革的，你也可以把它擦干净。(在软垫座椅上使用湿巾可能会导致座椅湿润，传播细菌，而不是杀死他们。)。
测量一个人的体温来寻找发烧的迹象并不像听起来那么容易，因为“正常”的体温数字可能会有所不同，但通常情况下，要注意100.5华氏度或更高的温度。如果你没有温度计(这些天它们可能很贵)，还有其他方法可以确定你是发烧了，还是有患新冠肺炎并发症的风险。
美国疾病控制与预防中心建议所有美国人在公共场合外出时都要戴上口罩。这是联邦指导方针的转变，反映了新的担忧，即冠状病毒是由没有症状的感染者传播的。到目前为止，疾控中心和世界卫生组织一样，建议普通人除非生病和咳嗽，否则不需要戴口罩。部分原因是为了保存医用级口罩，供医护人员在口罩持续供不应求的时候迫切需要。口罩不能取代洗手和社交距离。
如果你接触过冠状病毒或认为自己感染了冠状病毒，并出现发烧或咳嗽或呼吸困难等症状，请打电话给医生。他们应该就你是否应该接受检测，如何检测，以及如何在不潜在感染或暴露他人的情况下求医给你提供建议。
如果你生病了，你认为你已经接触了新的冠状病毒，疾控中心建议你打电话给你的医疗保健提供者，解释你的症状和恐惧。他们会决定你是否需要检查。请记住，有一种可能性--例如，因为缺乏检测工具，或者因为你没有症状--你可能无法接受检测。
他上周重返工作岗位，病人数量约为正常数量的四分之一。他于2017年从牙科学校毕业，担心如果他的案件量不能很快增加，他将如何偿还33万美元的未偿学生债务。
即使在上个月的工作岗位增加之后，牙科行业仍然比大流行前少了28.9万名工人。在经济学家史蒂文森看来，这表明该行业--以及美国经济的其他部分--还远未复苏。
她说：“牙科就业率下降了30%，这一事实告诉我们，收入会减少，人们会感到恐惧。”“我们可能看不到零售店的就业率恢复到去年的水平。但我们应该看到牙科就业一直回到原来的水平。“。
牙科行业-以及其他经济领域-的就业可能仍然会受到其他经济领域的限制，这些领域的重新开放速度不会那么快。对于托儿所和学校来说尤其如此，它们中的许多都不会在秋季重新全天开放。
德克萨斯州普莱诺31岁的儿科牙医艾比·阿迪耶(Abi Adeyeye)是5月份重返工作岗位的人之一。在过去的五周里，她看到患者数量反弹到冠状病毒前的水平，感到非常兴奋。
“在冠状病毒出现之前，我的取消率约为30%，”她说。“现在没有人取消了。看起来人们想要走出家门，需要做点什么。“。
即使病人日程排得满满的，她的办公室也没有充分就业。她过去有六名牙医助理，但现在只有四名回来工作了。其中一个怀孕了，还有一个无法获得托儿服务。
与此同时，牙科工作只会变得更具挑战性。Adeyeye医生现在戴着N95口罩、外科口罩、面罩和手术帽。
“头两周我有严重的偏头痛，”她说。“我不仅热，连呼吸都喘不过气来。”她正在慢慢适应新的牙科：“我的头痛已经降到每周一次。”
note_en:
If not for coronavirus, you’d expect your local dentist office to be doing just fine.
 Dentist offices tend to be stable businesses that stick around for decades, unlike restaurants that open and close frequently. Dentists earn a healthy salary — a median of  $159,000 — and offer services with no clear substitute. If you need your teeth cleaned or a cavity filled, the dentist is the only option.
 This makes them, in the eyes of some economists, the perfect barometer for gauging the country’s recovery from the shock of the pandemic.
 “If you look at your typical dentist office, nothing went wrong with their business model,” said Betsey Stevenson, an economics professor at the University of Michigan. “It’s just coronavirus that happened.”
 The dental industry has weathered an exaggerated version of the pandemic’s economic impact, experiencing both a steeper decline and a faster recovery than other sectors. Half of all dental workers lost their jobs in March and April as states closed businesses to slow the virus’s spread. The industry accounted for a staggering 35 percent of all health care jobs lost in those months, even though its workers make up just 6 percent of the industry, according to analysis of federal data by the nonprofit Altarum Institute.
  How long it takes those jobs to come back entirely will be a crucial indicator of whether Americans feel safe returning to normal activities, and if they have the economic means to do so.
 “I’m obsessed with dentists because, if the only thing we’re doing is putting the economy on pause, and then going back to normal, all of them should be coming back,” Ms. Stevenson said. “We’re not really recovered until all the dentists are back to work.”
  The dental industry halted much of its work on March 16, when the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention and the American Dental Association issued  joint guidance against elective care. Some dentists say they closed even earlier as protective equipment became in short supply.
 By mid-April, 45 percent of dentists had laid off their entire staffs, according to data collected by the dental association. Only 13 percent remained fully open, with the remaining offices keeping a skeleton staff. Patient visits fell to 7 percent of normal rates.
 Marko Vujicic, the chief economist at the dental association, expected a slow return of workers into dentist offices. But regular surveys, sent out to 12,000 dental practices every two weeks, showed a relatively fast recovery.
 “My initial predictions were we’d have an elevator ride down and an escalator ride up,” he said. “But we’re actually seeing a pretty sharp acceleration of the jobs coming back.”
 By early May, 33 percent of dental offices had hired their full staffs back. The number rose to 58 percent by mid-May and, most recently, hit 77 percent the first week of June.
  New federal data released last week tells a similar story. The dental industry gained a quarter-million jobs in May, accounting for a full 10 percent of the net jobs added across the American economy.
 Federal stimulus programs may have played a key role in bringing dentists back to work. An estimated 37 percent of dental offices received funding through the Paycheck Protection Program, meant to help small businesses keep workers on payroll. Dentist practices that participated in the program were more likely to remain open than those that didn’t.
 As dentists head back to work, it’s unclear whether patients will follow. While most states have given dentist offices the go-ahead to reopen, patient volumes remain half of what they were before the pandemic.
 That suggests it isn’t just stay-at-home orders that have caused patients to cancel appointments. Some may have lost the dental insurance they used to get at work. Others may fear contracting the virus; they may feel safer putting off preventive care that has already waited months. Or they may question the value of regular cleanings altogether.
 Dentists understand why coming into their offices — even with the extra protective equipment they’ve invested in — may not be an appealing proposition.
 “You have to have someone right in your face,” said Jason Bastida, who practices primarily in Elmhurst, a neighborhood in Queens that was hard hit by coronavirus. “I get to wear an N95 mask, but you have to make yourself vulnerable by taking your mask off.”
 The steroid, dexamethasone, is  the first  treatment shown to reduce mortality in severely ill patients, according to scientists in Britain. The drug appears to reduce inflammation caused by the immune system, protecting the tissues. In the study, dexamethasone reduced deaths of patients on ventilators by one-third, and deaths of patients on oxygen by one-fifth.
  The coronavirus emergency relief package gives many American workers paid leave if they need to take time off because of the virus. It gives qualified workers two weeks of paid sick leave if they are ill, quarantined or seeking diagnosis or preventive care for coronavirus, or if they are caring for sick family members. It gives 12 weeks of paid leave to people caring for children whose schools are closed or whose child care provider is unavailable because of the coronavirus.  It is the first time the United States has had widespread federally mandated paid leave, and includes people who don’t typically get such benefits, like part-time and gig economy workers. But  the measure excludes at least half of private-sector workers, including those at the country’s largest employers, and gives small employers significant leeway to deny leave.
  So far, the evidence seems to show it does. A widely cited  paper published in April suggests that people are most infectious about two days before the onset of coronavirus symptoms and estimated that 44 percent of new infections were a result of transmission from people who were not yet showing symptoms. Recently, a top expert at the World Health Organization stated that transmission of the coronavirus by people who did not have symptoms was “very rare,”  but she later walked back that statement.
  Touching contaminated objects and then infecting ourselves with the germs is not typically how the virus spreads. But it can happen. A number  of studies of flu, rhinovirus, coronavirus and other microbes have shown that respiratory illnesses, including the new coronavirus, can spread by touching contaminated surfaces, particularly in places like day care centers, offices and  hospitals. But a long chain of events has to happen for the disease to spread that way. The best way to protect yourself from coronavirus — whether it’s surface transmission or close human contact — is still social distancing, washing your hands, not touching your face and wearing masks.
  A study by European scientists is the first to document a strong statistical link between  genetic variations and Covid-19, the illness caused by the coronavirus.  Having Type A blood was linked to a 50 percent increase in the likelihood that a patient would need to get oxygen or to go on a ventilator, according to the new study.
  The unemployment rate fell to 13.3 percent in May, the Labor Department said on June 5, an unexpected improvement in the nation’s job market as hiring rebounded faster than economists expected. Economists had forecast the unemployment rate to increase to as much as 20 percent, after it hit 14.7 percent in April, which was the highest since the government began keeping official statistics after World War II. But the unemployment rate dipped instead, with employers adding 2.5 million jobs, after more than 20 million jobs were lost in April.
  Mass protests against police brutality that have brought thousands of people onto the streets in cities across America are raising the specter of new coronavirus outbreaks, prompting political leaders, physicians and public health experts to  warn that the crowds could cause a surge in cases. While many political leaders affirmed the right of protesters to express themselves, they urged the demonstrators to wear face masks and maintain social distancing, both to protect themselves and to prevent further community spread of the virus. Some infectious disease experts were reassured by the fact that the protests were held outdoors, saying the open air settings could mitigate the risk of transmission.
  States are reopening bit by bit. This means that more public spaces are available for use and more and more businesses are being allowed to open again. The federal government is largely leaving the decision up to states, and some state leaders are leaving the decision up to local authorities. Even if you aren’t being told to stay at home, it’s still a good idea to limit trips outside and your interaction with other people.
  Common symptoms  include fever, a dry cough, fatigue and difficulty breathing or shortness of breath. Some of these symptoms overlap with those of the flu, making detection difficult, but runny noses and stuffy sinuses are less common.  The C.D.C. has also added chills, muscle pain, sore throat, headache and a new loss of the sense of taste or smell as symptoms to look out for. Most people fall ill five to seven days after exposure, but symptoms may appear in as few as two days or as many as 14 days.
  If air travel is unavoidable,  there are some steps you can take to protect yourself. Most important: Wash your hands often, and stop touching your face. If possible, choose a window seat. A  study from Emory University found that during flu season, the safest place to sit on a plane is by a window, as people sitting in window seats had less contact with potentially sick people. Disinfect hard surfaces. When you get to your seat and your hands are clean, use disinfecting wipes to clean the hard surfaces at your seat like the head and arm rest, the seatbelt buckle, the remote, screen, seat back pocket and the tray table. If the seat is hard and nonporous or leather or pleather, you can wipe that down, too. (Using wipes on upholstered seats could lead to a wet seat and spreading of germs rather than killing them.)
  Taking one’s temperature to look for signs of fever is not as easy as it sounds, as “normal” temperature numbers can vary, but generally, keep an eye out for a temperature of 100.5 degrees Fahrenheit or higher. If you don’t have a thermometer (they can be pricey these days), there are  other ways to figure out if you have a fever, or are at risk of Covid-19 complications.
  The C.D.C. has  recommended that all Americans wear cloth masks if they go out in public. This is a shift in federal guidance reflecting  new concerns that the coronavirus is being spread by infected people who have no symptoms. Until now, the C.D.C., like the W.H.O., has advised that ordinary people don’t need to wear masks unless they are sick and coughing. Part of the reason was to preserve medical-grade masks for health care workers who desperately need them at a time when they are in continuously short supply. Masks don’t replace hand washing and social distancing.
  If you’ve been exposed to the coronavirus or think you have, and have a fever or symptoms like a cough or difficulty breathing, call a doctor. They should give you advice on whether you should be tested, how to get tested, and how to seek medical treatment without potentially infecting or exposing others.
  If you’re sick and you think you’ve been exposed to the new coronavirus,  the C.D.C. recommends that you call your healthcare provider and explain your symptoms and fears. They will decide if you need to be tested. Keep in mind that there’s a chance — because of a lack of testing kits or because you’re asymptomatic, for instance — you won’t be able to get tested.
 He returned to work last week and has about a quarter of his regular patient volume. He graduated from dental school in 2017, and worries about how he’ll pay off his $330,000 in outstanding student debt if his caseload doesn’t pick up soon.
 Even after last month’s job gains, the dental industry still has 289,000 fewer workers than it did before the pandemic. That suggests to Ms. Stevenson, the economist, that the industry — and the rest of the American economy — is far from recovered.
 “The fact that dentistry employment is down 30 percent tells us that there is income loss, and there is fear,” she said. “We might not see employment in a retail store get back to the levels it had last year. But we should see dental employment get all the way back to where it was.”
 Employment in the dental industry — and the rest of the economy — is likely to remain constrained by other areas of the economy that don’t reopen as quickly. This is especially true for day cares and schools, many of which will not reopen full time in the fall.
 Abi Adeyeye, a 31-year-old pediatric dentist in Plano, Tex., was among those who returned to work in May. Over the past five weeks, she has been excited to see patient volume rebound to pre-coronavirus levels.
 “Before coronavirus, I had a cancellation rate around 30 percent,” she said. “Now nobody cancels. It seems like people are wanting to get out of the house and need something to do.”
 Even with a full patient schedule, her office is not at full employment. She used to have six dental assistants, but only four have come back to work. One was pregnant, and one couldn’t secure child care.
 The work of dentistry, at the same time, has only become more challenging. Dr. Adeyeye now wears an N95 respirator mask, a surgical mask, a face shield and a surgical cap.
 “The first two weeks I had these massive migraines,” she said. “Not only am I hot, I also can’t breathe.” She’s slowly adjusting to the new dentistry: “My headaches have gone down to once a week.”
title: 经济状况如何？看牙医
title_en: How’s the Economy Doing? Watch the Dentists

note:
美国各州、县和地方政府正在逐步放松对新冠肺炎的限制，尽管病例又开始上升，全国感染率已超过200多万人。
不管你计划在多大程度上利用开商店、酒吧、餐馆和公园的机会，你都应该继续保持社交距离，在家外靠近其他人的时候戴上口罩，并保持同样的清洁习惯。导致新冠肺炎患病的冠状病毒SARS-CoV-2病毒不会很快消失，你需要保持对它的防御，无论多么温暖的天气和半繁忙的街道可能会让你感觉在某些地方，生活正在恢复正常。(编者注：非典冠状病毒2型病毒是导致新冠肺炎疾病的冠状病毒)不会很快消失，你需要保持对它的防御，无论温暖的天气和半繁忙的街道可能会让你感觉在某些地方，生活正在恢复正常。
阅读我们的冠状病毒齿轮和用品指南，开始在食品储藏室里装满罐头和必需品似乎更有成效，但家中表面的清洁和消毒可以帮助降低您或您所爱的人感染新冠肺炎的可能性，并降低您将其传染给其他人的可能性。让你的家(和自己)保持清洁对每个人都有好处。
疾病控制中心建议我们所有人都采取措施清洁和消毒家中触觉强烈的表面。下面，我们将深入讨论病毒可能在表面持续多长时间，哪些消毒剂可以杀死它，以及你应该采取哪些步骤来保持清洁。
更新6月11日：我们已经更新了我们的指南，以参考新发现的，冠状病毒引起的情况，这是影响一些儿童，称为儿童多系统炎症综合征(MIS-C)。我们还改变了我们的产品推荐，以反映价格和库存的变化。我们还增加了一些供应品的链接，尽管注意到高需求可能意味着它们的库存时有进货，也有缺货。
到目前为止，你已经听过一百万次了，而且你还会听到一百万次，但降低感染新冠肺炎的风险(或将其传染给其他人)的最好方法是在咳嗽、打喷嚏、摸脸、上厕所或即将离开一个地方去另一个地方后洗手。例如，当你离开和从杂货店回来时，你应该洗手。
如果你能找到的话，洗手液是一种非常有效的快速清洁方法。(下面是如何制作自己的衣服。)。不过，洗手液并不能代替用肥皂和水洗手。使用肥皂和水对你的手来说也会更容易一些。它不一定会杀死所有的病原体，但如果你正确洗手，它就会把它们洗掉。世界卫生组织有关于如何正确使用20秒洗手的详细说明(我们都以模因的形式看到过)。
给你的手大量滋润也很重要。干燥、龟裂的皮肤更容易受到各种感染，所以洗完后，涂上少许保湿霜。这真是太棒了！大多数保湿乳液都有相似的成分，从水和甘油开始，所以品牌并不重要。(以下是亚马逊上的一些洗手液。)。如果你的手特别干燥，可以找一些皮肤科医生推荐的带有强化标签的产品，比如尤塞林高级修复、露得清水提神或露得清挪威配方奶粉。
如果您在我们的故事中使用链接购买东西，我们可能会赚取佣金。这有助于支持我们的新闻事业。了解更多。
即使你没有生病，如果可以的话，就呆在家里吧。身处人山人海或外出就餐不仅会给你自己带来不必要的风险，也会给你周围的人带来不必要的风险。你在公共场合出现的次数越多，这种新型冠状病毒就越有可能搭便车在你的手上、衣服或人身上。数百万人非常容易感染这种病毒。把自己置于危险之中也会让他们处于危险之中。
将会有相当大一部分老年人或有其他健康状况的人，如果他们一下子生病了，他们将使医疗保健系统不堪重负。俄勒冈健康与科学大学感染预防与控制负责人约翰·汤斯博士告诉“连线”说，所以我们正在努力减少传播的数量。
在公共场所佩戴布质口罩。(下面是如何制作面具以及围绕面具的规则。)。
同样，勤洗手至少20秒(如果你不能洗手，也可以使用洗手液)。
向纸巾或手肘内侧咳嗽或打喷嚏。避免触摸你的脸。
疾控中心建议每个人在公共场合都戴上布质口罩。如果你找不到口罩，我们有详细的说明，如果你找不到口罩，我们会在我们的如何制作CDC批准的布面面膜指南中介绍口罩的使用方法和注意事项。
口罩是除了呆在家里、洗手、社交距离和其他你应该遵守的准则之外的额外预防措施。布口罩不太可能阻止你得新冠肺炎，但如果你碰巧得了这种病，它可能有助于保护其他人。一些患有这种疾病的人表现出轻微的症状，或者根本没有症状，特别是那些年轻的人，所以你或他们可能有这种病，但却不知道。据我们所知，这种新型冠状病毒是通过人与人之间的接触或呼吸道飞沫传播的。仅仅和某人交谈就可以把水滴送到他们的路上。
不要给2岁以下的孩子戴口罩，但要帮助他们与他人保持社交距离，并洗手。新的证据表明，孩子们特别容易受到另一种由接触冠状病毒引起的疾病的影响。医学专业人士将这种情况称为儿童多系统炎症综合征(MIS-C)。
不要购买N95口罩或任何可能用来帮助医护人员的专业口罩。美国各地口罩仍然严重短缺，N95口罩应该留给医疗专业人士。这就是为什么疾控中心再次建议你在家中自己制作布质口罩。
你首先要知道的是清洁和消毒是两码事。美国疾病控制与预防中心建议，即使你家里没有人生病，我们也要同时做这两件事。
人与人之间的传播比通过表面传播的风险要大得多，但疾控中心建议，为了安全起见，我们每天至少清洁和消毒家里高接触的表面一次，假设我们以某种方式与外部世界接触过，无论是一个人离开和返回，还是货物进来。
研究人员发现，这种新型冠状病毒能够在纸板等表面上存活24小时，但在塑料和不锈钢上最多能存活两三天。因此，即使我们不出门，清洁和消毒触感较高的表面也是我们都应该采取的步骤。任何时候物品或人进出你家，都有暴露的可能。
每个人的家都有一点不同，所以只要想想你最常接触的表面就知道了。对我来说，这包括上面的东西，加上桌子表面和鼠标垫(我们稍后会讲到小玩意儿)。现在你知道你要打扫的是什么了，下面就是你应该怎么做了。
首先，清洁表面，清除任何污染物、灰尘或杂物。你可以通过用肥皂水(或清洁喷雾)和手巾擦拭它们来做到这一点。
然后使用表面合适的消毒剂。要做到这一点，最快捷、最简单的方法是使用消毒湿巾或消毒剂喷雾。
就这样。只要把这些加到你的日常生活中，就可以帮助你和你家里的任何人降低感染的风险。如果你现在不能获得消毒剂，只需用你有的肥皂或清洁剂彻底处理即可。
EPA有一份可以杀死这种新型冠状病毒的消毒剂的完整清单，但这里有几个需要密切关注的要点。如果你当地的杂货店缺货，你可以在亚马逊或沃尔玛网上找到大多数消毒剂。大多数消毒剂都应该有一个标签，上面列出了它们对哪些病毒有效，这是你比任何特定的活性成分更想要注意的。
汤斯说，如果一种消毒剂产品有杀死流感、狂犬病、SARS病毒或其他冠状病毒的迹象，那么它也应该对这种病毒起作用。
尽管与3月和4月相比，许多商店对清洁用品的抢购已经开始缓解，但更多的货架仍然不少见。但你还有很多选择。首先，请多用肥皂、水和擦洗。这可能会带来巨大的不同。
疾控中心还推荐了一种使用家用漂白剂的自制清洁液配方。
喷洒于表面消毒，静置10分钟，用湿布擦拭干净。
漂白剂在大多数情况下都是过量的。你永远不应该把漂白剂和任何其他清洁剂混合在一起，它很可能会损坏或变色敏感的表面。如果你不能找到或获得任何其他类型的消毒剂，就把它作为最后的手段。使用漂白剂时，记得戴手套，打开窗户(通风是你的朋友)，并要小心。还有，拜托，拜托，别喝了。它只会让你生病。
或者，你也可以自己制作不含漂白剂的消毒剂喷雾，里面有一些你可以在网上订购的配料。
是的，基本上是这样。只需用普通洗衣皂洗衣服，然后用比其他温度稍高一点的温度烘干，这就是给衣服消毒所要做的全部事情。
一定要消毒脏衣服接触到的表面，包括篮子和你的手-特别是如果你家里有病人的话。
像清洗其他任何表面一样清洁和消毒篮子，在处理完病人的脏衣服后要彻底洗手。疾控中心建议在你的篮子里使用衬垫。
别忘了清理你的外套和背包。除非你的夹克是可以机洗的，否则用消毒湿巾擦拭里面应该是管用的。
不，不是没有原因的。根据FDA的说法，没有证据表明食品或食品包装可以传播这种新型冠状病毒，因此目前没有必要比平时更多地对食品或食品包装进行消毒。只要遵守食品安全标准，事后洗手即可。
是的，淡淡的。根据美国食品和药物管理局的说法，邮件和包裹传播这种新型冠状病毒的风险相对较低。也就是说，研究人员发现它可以在纸板上存活大约24小时，所以用消毒纸巾擦拭一下包裹并不是个坏主意。
这就是清洁和消毒可能会变得棘手的地方。在自我隔离期间，你的设备可能是让你保持清醒的唯一因素，但我们都知道，它们是细菌的磁铁。它们是高度接触的表面，你可以随身携带，所以你也需要清洁和消毒它们。为了避免重复我的话，让我们在这里只说一句：消毒湿巾是清洗你的设备的最好方法，毫无疑问。但有些设备有特殊的考虑因素。
如果你有的话，用消毒湿巾或酒精溶液给iPhone或Android手机消毒(至少70%)。一定要特别注意屏幕，按钮，以及任何灰尘和口袋里的绒线容易被困住的地方。还要确保你拿掉手机或平板电脑上的所有保护套，清洁底部，放回原处，并清洁外部。按照美国疾病控制与预防中心(CDC)对家中其他高接触表面的建议，每天消毒一次不会对你的设备造成伤害。
笔记本电脑的显示屏并不总是由玻璃制成的(哑光显示屏是塑料的)，所以避免在屏幕上使用消毒擦拭，以防万一。显示器应用异丙醇(70%)溶液和软毛巾清洁。确保将键盘、触摸板、外部和手腕放在笔记本电脑上的位置擦拭干净。
大多数台式电脑已经非常需要清洗了。最好的方法是用消毒湿巾或异丙醇溶液和一条柔软的毛巾。同样，避免消毒监视器上的湿巾，以防万一-坚持使用异丙醇。否则，只需确保擦拭鼠标(顶部、侧面和底部)、键盘上的按键、键盘外部以及您可能拥有的任何鼠标垫即可。
对于任何其他电子设备，如果外部大部分是塑料的(游戏鼠标、游戏手柄、电视遥控器)，用消毒湿巾或异丙醇溶液快速检查一下是安全的。
现在有很多事情要做。压力很大。这太可怕了。可能很难知道你应该做什么，或者正在发生什么。如果你有更多的问题，现在谁没有问题，我们有很多关于这种新型冠状病毒的深思熟虑、深入研究的新闻和文章。您可以在这里阅读更多内容。在外面保持安全，如果可以的话，请呆在家里。
“连线”正在免费提供有关公共卫生以及如何在冠状病毒大流行期间保护自己的报道。订阅我们的冠状病毒更新时事通讯以获取最新更新，并订阅以支持我们的新闻。
note_en:
States, counties, and local governments are gradually loosening Covid-19 restrictions across the US, despite the fact that  cases are beginning to rise again and the nationwide infection rate has surpassed 2 million individuals.
 Regardless of how much you plan to take advantage of opening shops, bars, restaurants, and parks, you should continue social distancing, using a mask when near others outside your home, and maintaining the same cleaning habits. The SARS-CoV-2 virus–the coronavirus that causes the disease Covid-19–isn't going away anytime soon, and you need to keep up your defenses against it, no matter how much warm weather and semi-bustling streets may lull you into feeling like life is, in some places, returning to normal.
 It might seem more productive to read our  Coronavirus Gear and Supplies Guide and start filling your pantry with canned goods and essentials, but cleaning and sanitizing surfaces in your home can help lower the chances you or a loved one will contract Covid-19 and lower the chances you might spread it to someone else. Keeping your home (and self) sanitized helps everyone.
  The Centers for Disease Control  recommends we all take steps to clean and sanitize high-touch surfaces in our homes. Below, we get into the weeds of how long the virus might last on surfaces, which disinfectants may kill it, and the steps you should take to keep clean.
 Updated June 11: We've updated our guide to reference the newly discovered, coronavirus-caused condition that's affecting some children, known as multisystem inflammatory syndrome in children (MIS-C). We've also changed our product recommendations to reflect pricing and stock changes. We've also added links to some supplies, though note that high demand could mean they go in and out of stock.
   You’ve heard it a million times by now, and you’ll hear it a million more, but the best way to lower your risk of contracting Covid-19 (or pass it on to someone else) is to  wash your hands after you cough, sneeze, touch your face, use the restroom, or are about to leave one place for another. You should wash your hands when you leave and return from the grocery store, for instance.
  If you can find any, hand sanitizer is a fast cleaning method that works wonders. ( Here's how to make your own.) Hand sanitizer is no substitute for washing your hands with soap and water, though. Using soap and water can also be a little easier on your hands. It won't necessarily kill all pathogens, but if you wash your hands properly, it'll wash them away. The World Health Organization has detailed instructions (which we've all seen in meme form) on how to  properly perform the 20-second hand wash.
  It’s also important to  liberally moisturize your hands. Dry, cracked skin is at greater risk for all kinds of infections, so after you wash, apply a little moisturizer. It's nice! Most moisturizing lotions have similar ingredients, starting with water and glycerin, so the brand doesn't really matter. (Here are  some hand lotions on Amazon.) If your hands are extra dry, look for something dermatologist-recommended with an "intensive" label, like  Eucerin Advanced Repair,  Neutrogena Hydro Boost, or  Neutrogena Norwegian Formula
 If you buy something using links in our stories, we may earn a commission. This helps support our journalism.  Learn more.
  Even if you're not sick, just stay home if you can. Being in large crowds or going out to restaurants poses unnecessary risks not just to yourself but to the people around you. The more you're in public, the more chances the novel coronavirus has to hitch a ride on your hands, clothes, or person.  Millions of people are very vulnerable to this virus. Putting yourself at risk also puts them at risk.
  "There will be a sizable portion of people who are older or who have other health conditions, and if they get sick all at once, they're going to overwhelm the health care system. So we're trying to decrease the number of transmissions," Dr. John Townes, head of infection prevention and control at the Oregon Health & Science University, told WIRED.
  Wear a cloth face mask in public. ( Here's how to make a mask and the rules surrounding them.)
  Again, wash your hands frequently for at least 20 seconds (or use  hand sanitizer if you're unable to wash).
 Cough or sneeze into a tissue or the inside of your elbow. Avoid touching your face.
  The CDC recommends that everyone  wear a cloth face mask in public. We have detailed instructions, and do's and don'ts, on masks in our  How to Make a CDC-Approved Cloth Face Mask guide if you're unable to find one to buy.
 Masks are an added precaution on top of staying home, washing your hands, social distancing, and the other guidelines that you should be following. A cloth mask is unlikely to prevent you from getting Covid-19, but it may help protect others if you happen to have the disease. Some people who have the disease show mild symptoms, or none at all—particularly, those who are young–so you or they may have it and not know. As far as we're aware, the novel coronavirus is transmitted through person-to-person contact or  respiratory droplets. Just talking to someone can send droplets their way.
 Do not put a mask on kids under 2 years old, but do help them social distance from others, and wash their hands. New evidence suggests kids are  especially vulnerable to another condition caused by exposure to the coronavirus. Medical professionals have termed this condition  Multisystem Inflammatory Syndrome in Children (MIS-C).
  Don't buy an N95 mask or any professional mask that could be used to help health care workers. There is still a severe shortage of masks around the United States, and N95 masks should be reserved for medical professionals. This is why the CDC, again, recommends you make your own cloth mask at home.
   The first thing you'll want to know is that cleaning and disinfecting are two very different things. The CDC recommends we all do a bit of both,  even if nobody in your home is sick.
  Transmission from person-to-person is a much greater risk than transmission via surfaces, but the CDC recommends we clean and disinfect high-touch surfaces in our homes at least once daily just to be safe, assuming we have had contact with the outside world in some way, either a person leaving and returning or goods coming in.
  Researchers have found that the novel coronavirus is  capable of living on surfaces such as  cardboard for 24 hours, but up to two or three days on plastic and stainless steel. So cleaning and disinfecting high-touch surfaces is a step we should all take, even if we aren't leaving the house. Anytime items or people come in and out of your home, there is some possibility of exposure.
  Everyone’s home is a little different, so just think about the surfaces you interact with most. For me, that includes the above, plus desk surfaces and mousepads (we'll get to gadgets in a bit). Now that you know what you're cleaning, here's how you should do it.
  First, clean the surfaces, removing any contaminants, dust, or debris. You can do this by wiping them with soapy water (or a cleaning spray) and a hand towel.
 Then apply a surface-appropriate disinfectant. The quickest and easiest way to do this is with disinfecting wipes or disinfectant spray.
 That’s it. Just adding these to your daily routine can help lower the risk of infection for you and anyone else in your household. If you aren't able to obtain disinfectants at this time, just do a thorough job with the soap or cleaning agents you do have.
  The  EPA has a full list of disinfectants that will kill the novel coronavirus, but here are a few essentials to keep an eye out for. You can find most of these  disinfectants online at Amazon or  Walmart if your local grocery store is out of stock. Most disinfectants should have a label that lists the viruses they're effective against, and that's what you'll want to look out for more than any particular active ingredient.
 "If a disinfectant product has an indication for killing influenza, RSB, SARS virus, or other coronaviruses, then it should work against this one also," Townes said.
    Although the rush on cleaning supplies has begun to ease in many stores compared to March and April, barer shelves are still not uncommon. But you still have plenty of options. First off, please do use more soap, water, and scrubbing. That can make a huge difference.
 The CDC also has a  recommended recipe for a homemade cleaning solution using household bleach.
  Spray on surface to disinfect, let sit for 10 minutes, wipe away with wet cloth
 Bleach is excessive in most cases. You should never ever mix bleach solution with any other cleaning chemical, and it's likely to damage or discolor sensitive surfaces. Use it as a last resort if you can't source or acquire any other kind of disinfectant. With bleach, remember to wear gloves, open your windows (ventilation is your friend), and be careful. And please, please,  please,  don't drink it. It'll only make you sick.a
  Alternatively you can  make your own bleach-free sanitizer spray with a few ingredients you can order online.
   Yes, mostly. Just washing your clothing with regular laundry soap and drying it at a slightly higher temperature than you might have otherwise is all you have to do to disinfect your clothes.
  Be sure to  disinfect surfaces the dirty laundry comes in contact with, including the hamper and your hands—especially if you have a sick person in the house.
 Clean and disinfect the hamper like you would any other surface, and wash your hands thoroughly after handling dirty laundry from someone who is ill. The CDC recommends using a liner in your hamper.
 Don't forget to clean your coat and backpack. Wiping the inside off with a disinfectant wipe should do the trick unless your jacket is machine washable.
  No, not without reason. According to the FDA, there is  no evidence to suggest that food or food packaging can transmit the novel coronavirus, so there is currently no need to disinfect food or food packaging any more than you usually would. Just observe standard food safety and wash your hands afterward.
  Yes, lightly.  According to the USPS, mail and packages are relatively low-risk for transmitting the novel coronavirus. That said, researchers have found that  it can live on cardboard for around 24 hours, so giving packages a once over with a disinfecting wipe isn't a bad idea.
   Here’s where cleaning and disinfecting can get tricky. Your devices might be all that’s keeping you sane during your self-isolation but, as we all know, they’re magnets for germs. They’re high-touch surfaces you carry with you everywhere, so you need to clean and disinfect them, too. To avoid repeating myself, let’s just say it here: Disinfecting wipes are the best way to clean your devices, hands down. But some devices have special considerations.
  If you have them, disinfect an iPhone or Android phone with a disinfecting wipe or alcohol solution (at least 70 percent). Make sure you pay special attention to the screen, the buttons, and anywhere dust and pocket lint tend to get trapped. Also make sure you remove any case that’s on your phone or tablet, clean underneath, put it back on, and clean the outside. Following the CDC recommendations for other high-touch surfaces in the home, a once-daily disinfecting isn’t going to hurt your devices.
   Laptop displays aren’t always made of glass (matte displays are plastic) so avoid using a disinfecting wipe on the screen, just in case. The display should be cleaned with isopropyl alcohol (70 percent) solution and a soft towel. Make sure you wipe down the keyboard, the trackpad, the exterior, and where your wrists rest on the laptop.
  Most desktop computers are already in sore need for a cleaning. The best way to do that is with a disinfecting wipe or isopropyl alcohol solution and a soft towel. Again, avoid disinfecting wipes on the monitor, just in case—stick to isopropyl alcohol there. But otherwise, just make sure you wipe down the mouse (top, sides, and bottom), the keys on your keyboard, the exterior of the keyboard, and any mousepad you might have.
  For any other electronic device, if the exterior is largely plastic (gaming mice, gamepads, TV remotes) it’s safe to give them a once-over with a disinfecting wipe or isopropyl alcohol solution.
  There's a lot going on right now. It's stressful. It's scary. It can be hard to know what you should do or what's going on. If you have more questions, and who doesn't right now, we have a lot of thoughtful, thoroughly researched news and articles about the novel coronavirus. You can  read more here. Stay safe out there, and please, if you can, stay home.
  WIRED is providing  free access to stories about public health and how to protect yourself during the  coronavirus pandemic. Sign up for our  Coronavirus Update newsletter for the latest updates, and  subscribe to support our journalism.
title: 如何清洁和消毒您自己、您的家和您的物品
title_en: How to Clean and Disinfect Yourself, Your Home, and Your Stuff

Cervus在Linux内核(试图遵循CommonWA规范)之上实现了WebAssembly&34；usermode&34；，使wasm应用程序能够直接在环0中运行，同时仍然确保安全性。
Cervus实现了CommonWA的大部分(在#2中跟踪)，其示例可以在cwa-rs/examples中找到。
我一直忙于高考，直到2018年6月10日，在此之前我不能积极维护这个项目。但是，有几件事可以相对容易地处理：
通过实现后端特征与系统的其余部分交互，位于src/backend/exagon_e中的基于解释器的后端就是一个很好的示例。
Cervus本身必须使用GPL 2.0许可证，因为它链接到Linux内核。但是，在Cervus上运行的用户代码不受此限制。
note_en:
Cervus implements a WebAssembly "usermode" on top of the Linux kernel (which tries to follow the  CommonWA specification), enabling wasm applications to run directly in ring 0, while still ensuring safety and security.
                 Cervus implements most of  CommonWA (tracked at  #2), whose examples can be found at  cwa-rs/examples.
        I'm busy with my College Entrance Examination until ~June 10, 2018, before which I cannot actively maintain this project. However, there are a few things that can be relatively easily worked on:
   Interface with the rest of the system by implementing the  Backend trait, for which the interpreter-based backend located in  src/backend/hexagon_e is a good example to start with.
    Cervus itself has to use the GPL 2.0 license because it links to the Linux kernel. However, user code that runs on Cervus is not limited by this.
title: Cervus-Linux的WebAssembly子系统(2018)
title_en: Cervus – A WebAssembly Subsystem for Linux (2018)

note:
关于搜索阅读这本书我从询问我的眼科医生开始了我的探索，他热情地提供了大量令人眼花缭乱的技术信息，这些信息是为不同的受众和眼睛状况设计的各种眼表和测试。突然，一个简单的问题变成了一系列的发现。不仅有一种字形设计或字体用于眼表，字形设计更恰当的是被称为视标，有几种不同的版本。视标的设计和它们在特定距离上的清晰度是一门科学。由于我是一名平面设计师，而不是眼睛或视觉专家，我将放弃技术解释，将重点放在几个重要图表上使用的视标上，以更好地理解这个复杂而迷人的主题。
眼表是用来测试视力或视力清晰度的。根据临床环境、患者概况和诊断目标，每个图表设计都有局限性和优势。要了解这些图表之间的差异，了解一些标准化视力测试的历史背景是有帮助的。
德国眼科医生库克勒在1836年设计了一张图表，使用的是从日历、书籍和报纸上剪切下来的数字，这些数字被粘合成一排排减小尺寸的胶水在纸上。这些数字包括大炮、枪、鸟、农具、骆驼和青蛙。这个系统是有限的，因为图形在视觉重量或风格上不一致。库克勒博士继续改进他的图表，并在1843年出版了一个新版本，使用了12行减小大小的黑信字母。这张图表没有被广泛采用(很难想象为什么)，而且在1843年只出版了一次。1个。
视力表设计的下一个重大发展是斯内伦眼表，大多数美国人在去车管所时都能认出它。斯内伦眼表是由荷兰眼科医生赫尔曼·斯内伦于1862年设计的，目的是改善视力测试的主观性质，这通常是通过让患者手持或由医生举着阅读一段文字来完成的。这项测试有明显的局限性：结果取决于患者的阅读能力，所用字体的可读性，以及患者可以通过阅读句子猜测下一个单词的事实。据史密斯-凯特韦尔眼科研究所的科学家、眼图设计专家奥古斯特·科伦布兰德博士称，斯内伦开始在他的眼图中试验丁蝙蝠，也就是方块和圆圈等符号，但发现测试对象很难准确描述这些符号。2个。
因此，他开始使用字母。第一张斯内伦图上的字符是：A，C，E，G，L，N，P，R，T，5，V，Z，B，D，4，F，H，K，O，S，3，U，Y，A，C，E，G和L，使用的字母是埃及Paragon或线条粗细对比的板条，末端有装饰十字。斯内伦随后推断，测试对象将能够更容易地识别视觉大小一致的非装饰性单行/等重字母，因此他创造了视标。3个。
乍一看，斯内伦的视标似乎是卢巴林图或洛克韦尔。但仔细观察，很明显，这些人物是相当不典型的。与字母比例由“家族”分组(如n、r、m、h和u)确定的典型字体不同，Snellen眼镜式字体设计在5x5的网格上。此外，它们包含的字符集非常有限，只有9-10个字母。光学字体是使用简单的几何形状设计的，其中线条的重量等于线条之间的负间距。光版的高度和宽度是线条粗细的五倍。4.。
这些设计考虑因素造成的字母比例参差不齐。例如，在典型字体中，C和D看起来比Z宽，但在视标方案中，情况正好相反。斯内伦博士使用弧度分钟代替排版测量系统创建了OptoTypes。这使得他的图表很容易被复制。斯内伦图表的第一笔大订单是1863年来自英国军队的。从那时起，斯内伦眼表成为视力测试的标准将近一个世纪。此外，斯奈伦的5x5栅格视标设计是所有其他眼图系统的基础。斯内伦眼图仍然是最受认可的设计，这在某种程度上可以否定它的有效性，例如，如果测试对象已经记住了该表的话。5个。
大多数斯内伦图表包含11行大写字母。第一行由单个大字母组成，最常见的是E。后续行的字母数量不断增加，大小逐渐变小。测试对象从20英尺远的地方遮住一只眼睛，从顶部开始，大声朗读每一行的字母。微微一笑
当前的斯内伦图表使用九个字母，C，D，E，F，L，O，P，T，Z。请注意，除了E和O之外，所有的字母都是辅音。视标的不同形状使受试者能够识别垂直、水平和对角线。这些字母形状在识别散光方面也非常有效。
尽管今天的斯内伦眼图在行数、大小等级和衬线或无衬线设计上可能有所不同，7。
它们的共同点包括矩形。这规定了在空间允许的情况下出现在每行上的不同数量的视标。8由于不断改进，今天的大多数斯内伦图表都遵循对数级数，改进了字母设计，线条之间的行进幅度均为25%。9个。
1868年，密苏里州圣路易斯内科和外科医生学院的约翰·格林博士决定对斯内伦眼表做一些修改。他设计了一种更有结构的网格，其特征是连续线条的对数几何级数一致，为25%，并且间距成比例。他还改变了光字的风格，从块状的板条衬线改为无衬线。他的概念被称为“首选数字系列”，但他的系统直到下个世纪无衬线印刷术流行起来才得到广泛认可。具有讽刺意味的是，为了回应批评他的信件看起来“未完成”的批评，格林博士在1872年抛弃了它们，回到了衬线光印。10个。
1959年，约翰·霍普金斯大学的路易丝·斯隆博士用格林博士喜欢的无衬线字母创造了10种新的视标。这些视标包括字母C、D、H、K、N、O、R、S、V和Z。与斯奈伦字母一样，斯隆字母也是在正方形内形成的，笔划宽度等于字母高度的五分之一，具有相同的视觉粗细。斯隆图表的字母和行之间的间距与字母大小成正比。字母之间的间距等于字母宽度，行之间的间距等于后续较小行中字母的高度。11个。
请注意，与在斯内伦图表中一样，除了0之外，所有字符都是辅音。还要注意，斯内伦图表上使用的字母选择与斯隆图表中使用的字母选择不同。在这两种情况下，光版的不同形状允许测试对象识别垂直、水平和对角线-这有助于识别或区分单个字母。十封斯隆书信被认为是最有效的字母选择，具有同等的易读性。更重要的是，它们在识别散光方面特别有效。，但仔细检查后，再一次明显地发现，强加在这些视标上的网格格式产生了一些奇怪且比例不一致的字母。1976年，澳大利亚国家视力研究所的Ian Bailey和Jan E Lovie-Kitchin提出了一种新的图表布局，他们的概念如下：
我们设计了一系列近视觉图表，其中选择了字体、大小顺序、大小范围、每行字数和间距，以努力实现测试任务的标准化。
此布局将每行字母数可变的Snellen矩形图表格式替换为每行五个按比例间隔的字母的三角形图表格式。10个斯隆光学字体出现在贝利-洛维图表上，字母高度的字母比例等于5个笔划宽度，不包括衬线。
贝利-洛维图表是LogMAR测试的一个例子，这个术语描述了用来表示视觉敏锐度的几何符号。1984年，国际眼科委员会选择了“最小分辨率对数”(LogMAR)测试作为视力测试的标准。13个。
1982年，当国家眼科研究所的“糖尿病视网膜病变早期治疗研究”(ETDRS)需要标准化图表时，Rick Ferris博士将Green和Bailey-Lovie图表的对数级数和格式与斯隆信函结合起来。ETDRS图表使用相等的字母和线条间距，使视力图表更加平衡。14.。
这种图表格式已经被国家眼科研究所和FDA接受，并被授权在全球范围内进行许多临床试验。ETDRS测试比Snellen或Sloan版本更准确，因为这些行包含相同数量的字母，行和字母在对数刻度上等间距，并且单个行在字母难度上是平衡的。还有三种不同版本的测试可以用来阻止记忆。15个。
最初的ETDRS图表的一个局限性是它使用的是拉丁字母，这使得它很难在整个欧洲使用。为了解决这一限制，翻滚的E和Landolt C图表用于不熟悉拉丁字母的人群。最近，使用拉丁字母、希腊语和西里尔字母开发了一个改进的ETDRS图表。对于此图表，标准字母C、D、N、R、S、V和Z已替换为字母E、P、X、B、T、M和A。这些字母是使用相同的5 x 5栅格和斯隆字母创建的。16个。
最近几年，出现了创建电子图表的趋势，包括英国设计的测试图表2000，这是世界上第一个基于Windows的计算机化测试图表。它克服了几个难题，如屏幕对比度，并提供了改变字母顺序的机会，使其无法记忆。17.。
这些适用于Mac和Windows操作系统的字体可用于研究目的。这些字体基于路易丝·斯隆的设计，该字体已被美国国家科学院、国家研究委员会和视觉委员会指定为美国视力测试标准。18岁。
对于不识字的患者或不熟悉拉丁字母的患者，可以使用翻滚E眼图和兰道特C或断环图。19个。
翻滚E图是由澳大利亚眼科研究中心(CERA)的休·泰勒教授于1978年设计的，目的是测试澳大利亚原住民的视力，试图识别那些患有沙眼的人。
泰勒教授使用斯内伦比例，设计了一个类似大写E的形状，他在四个方向(上、下、右和左)排列，尺寸逐渐变小。然后，病人简单地描述翻滚的E面朝哪个方向。
兰道特C或破环眼图也适用于文盲或不熟悉拉丁字母的人。这项测试由瑞士眼科医生埃德蒙·兰道特发明，现在被认为是欧洲的标准。断环(与斯内伦和斯隆图表中的C的比例相同)以90°为增量旋转。C间隙的最小可感知角度是视力的测量。20个。
除了翻滚E和兰道特C测试之外，还有一些儿童图表，其中使用的是逐渐变小的、简单的物体象形图。设计这些图表的挑战是创建视觉重量相等、风格一致和设计一致的可辨认的象形图。
这篇文章并不是对眼表的主题或它们的功效进行详尽的研究。眼图的例子还有很多。我的目标是探索眼表作为一种诊断工具的演变过程中视标设计的原型。现在我可以告诉我的学生，从技术上讲，没有一种字体可以推荐给他们的设计；我可以让他们参考这篇文章以了解更多信息！检查验光仪是一种让人大开眼界的经历。
脚注：1.。“库克勒图表”，2015年6月10日，↑2。“那张眼图是谁做的？”，2015年6月8日，↑3。“照顾好你的眼睛”，2015年6月13日，↑4。“斯内伦眼图”，2015年6月6日，↑5。“那张眼图是谁做的？”，2015年6月7日，↑6。“眼图与20/20远景”，2015年6月12日，↑7。“照顾好你的眼睛”，6月15日收看，2015年↑8。“斯内伦眼图”，2015年6月7日，↑9。“斯内伦信函行进展”，2015年6月8日，↑10。“格林博士的发现”，2015年6月4日，↑11。“斯隆书信”，2015年7月2日，↑12。“ETDRS测试”，↑13,2015年6月7日访问。“LogMAR测试”，[pdf]访问2014年7月2日↑14。“ETDRS测试”，↑15,2015年6月7日访问。“ETDRS测试”，访问时间为2015年6月10日↑16。“修改后的ETDRS测试”，2015年6月10日访问，↑17。“修改后的ETDRS测试”，2015年6月12日，↑18。“斯隆书信”，2015年6月10日，↑19。“翻滚E测试”，2015年6月12日，↑20。↑Lorrie Frear是纽约罗切斯特理工学院设计学院/成像艺术与科学学院的副教授，在那里她教授平面设计和书法。洛里还在全国范围内举办书法和刻字讲座和研讨会，这些讲座和研讨会是根据观众的需求和兴趣量身定做的。她的专业和学生工作可以在教员网站上查看。
